game_name: Glory of Heracles IV: Gift from the Gods

design_philosophy.core_loop: Party-based turn combat exploration with Greek mythology narrative progression
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Spiritual/metaphysical conflict - protagonist's spirit separated from body, navigating Greek mythological trials
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Experience Greek mythology as a spiritually displaced hero, wielding party mechanics and special abilities unique to spirit form
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Loose narrative adaptation of Greek mythology emphasizing Atlantis and Pandora's Box mythology rather than strict canon; spirit-based protagonist enables unique mechanical interactions unavailable to ordinary characters

progression.upgrade_system: Party leveling system with progression-based character stat growth
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-driven progression unlocking new party members and abilities as narrative advances
replayability_hooks: Multiple party composition options enabling different tactical approaches
Greek mythology exploration encouraging secondary quest discovery

world_building_techniques: Greek mythological setting with loose adaptation of canonical episodes
Atlantis lore integration into plot structure
Pandora's Box narrative framework as central plot device
Spirit-form protagonist creating thematic coherence with metaphysical worldbuilding

boss_design.philosophy: Mythologically-grounded encounters reflecting Greek pantheon and legendary creatures; spirit-form protagonist may enable unconventional approaches

faction_indicators: Greek gods and divine entities as ally/adversary framework
Atlantis civilizational powers opposing player progression
Pandora's Box corruption spreading through world
environmental_storytelling: Greek mythology locations reimagined through spirit-perception mechanics
Atlantis ruins revealing ancient civilization collapse
Pandora's Box manifestations creating corrupted zones
